incidents.event: At least 29 commercial ships, including 13 tankers, attacked or reported incidents in the Gulf, Strait of Hormuz, or Gulf of Oman since March 1, 2026. Iranian fast-attack craft shadowed several vessels causing temporary halt in traffic.
Seized by Iran's IRGC Navy in the Strait of Hormuz after being fired upon; crew detained; vessel immobilized in Iranian waters.
Seized by Iran's IRGC Navy in the Strait of Hormuz for allegedly operating without necessary permits; vessel moved into Iranian territorial waters; crew held for investigative questioning.
U.S. President Donald Trump ordered US Navy to 'shoot and kill' any vessels suspected of laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating military posture.
Fired upon by IRGC gunboat while transiting Strait of Hormuz; no damage or injuries; vessel continued towards Gulf of Oman.
Iran threatened to sink US ships if Washington attempts to police the Strait of Hormuz, escalating military tensions.
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) formally closed the Strait of Hormuz to commercial traffic, issuing warnings against any vessels attempting passage.

Iranian authorities seized two container vessels, detaining approximately 40 crew members. Vessel traffic collapsed from 120-140 daily transits to as few as 3-6 vessels per day. Escalating US-Iran tensions led to withdrawal of war-risk insurance coverage and US naval forces diverted approximately 33 commercial vessels.
U.S. Navy seized the Iranian-flagged oil tanker TOUSKA in the Indian Ocean amid rising tensions.
